zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq於1477年出生在英國爾伊頓時，他的父親Robert Grosvenor32歲，母親Katherine NORREYS41歲。他於1495年與Katherine Cotton在英國英格蘭爾伊頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他於1621年住在英國爾且於1621年住在英國爾艾克斯頓。他去世於1542年7月27日在英國爾伊頓享年65歲，埋葬在英國爾艾克斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq于1477年出生在英国爾伊頓时，他的父亲Robert Grosvenor32岁，母亲Katherine NORREYS41岁。他于1495年与Katherine Cotton在英国英格兰爾伊頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他于1621年住在英国爾且于1621年住在英国爾艾克斯頓。他去世于1542年7月27日在英国爾伊頓享年65岁，埋葬在英国爾艾克斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Grosvenor,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from French gros ‘big, great’ + veneur ‘hunter’.

History: This is the name of one of the wealthiest families in Britain, which holds the title Duke of Westminster. They have been long established in Cheshire, with strong links with the city of Chester. One of the earliest recorded bearers of the name was Robert le Grosvenor of Budworth, who was granted lands by the Earl of Chester in 1160. The family's fortunes were founded by Thomas Grosvenor (born 1656), who in 1677 married an heiress, Mary Davies, whose inheritance included Ebury Farm, Middlesex. This now forms an area of central London that includes Grosvenor Square and Belgrave Square.

variants.name: Gravenor
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French grand ‘big, tall’ + veneor ‘hunter’. Compare Grosvenor .

nameMeanings.name: Esq
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone living on or owning land that was waterlogged or partly surrounded by water, from Middle Low German es ‘swamp, water’.
Swiss German: from the topographic name Ess ‘grazing area, pasture’.
Swiss German: from a vernacular short form of the personal name   Oswald  .

variants.name: Esse
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from the farmstead Ese in Sogn of unexplained etymology, or Esse in Telemark, named with Old Norse æsin ‘hill’ + -e from vin ‘meadow’.
German: variant of   Ess  , or a topographic name from the river name Esse in Hesse.

variants.name: Oswald
meanings: Scottish (Fife and Lanarkshire), northern English, German, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from an Old English personal name composed of the elements ōs ‘god’ + weald ‘power’. In the Middle English period, this fell together with the less common Old Norse cognate Ásvaldr. The name was introduced to Germany from England, as a result of the fame of Saint Oswald, a 7th-century king of Northumbria, whose deeds were reported by Celtic missionaries to southern Germany. The name was also borne by a 10th-century English saint of Danish parentage, who was important as a monastic reformer. Veneration of Saint Oswald, the king, spread from the German lands to the neighbouring Slavic lands as well. The surname in the (German) spelling Oswald is thus also found especially in Czechia and Slovakia, while in North America it also absorbed various Slavic forms (see 3 below).
Irish (Down): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa (see Hussey 1).

Americanized form of Slovenian, Slovak, and Czech Osvald, Slovenian and Slovak Ožvald or Ozvald, and probably also of Slovenian Ožbolt: from vernacular forms of the German personal name Oswald, of Old English origin (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq was born in 1477, in Eaton, Cheshire, England, his father, Robert Grosvenor, was 32 and his mother, Katherine NORREYS, was 41. He married Katherine Cotton in 1495, in Eaton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 9 daughters. He lived in Cheshire, England in 1621 and Eccleston, Cheshire, England in 1621. He died on 27 July 1542, in Eaton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Eccleston, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq est né en 1477, à Eaton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Grosvenor, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Katherine NORREYS, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Cotton en 1495, à Eaton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 9 filles. Il a vécu à Cheshire, Angleterre en 1621 et à Eccleston, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1621. Il est décédé le 27 juillet 1542, à Eaton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Eccleston, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq 1477 in Eaton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Grosvenor 32 und seine Mutter Katherine NORREYS 41. Er hat 1495 in Eaton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Katherine Cotton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 9 Töchter. 1621 hat er in Cheshire, England und 1621 in Eccleston, Cheshire, England gelebt. Er starb am 27. Juli 1542 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Eaton, Cheshire, England und wurde in Eccleston, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq è nato il nel 1477 nel Eaton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Grosvenor, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Katherine NORREYS, aveva 41 anni. Lui sposato Katherine Cotton nel 1495, nel Eaton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 9 figlie. Vissuto a Cheshire, England nel 1621 e nel Eccleston, Cheshire, England nel 1621. Morì il 27 luglio 1542, nel Eaton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Eccleston, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esqが1477年にイギリスチェシュアイアエアトンで生まれた時，父Robert Grosvenorは32歳，母Katherine NORREYSは41歳であった。1495年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアエアトンでKatherine Cottonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1621年にはイギリスチェシュアイアに，1621年にはイギリスチェシュアイアエククレストンに住んでいた。彼は1542年7月27日にイギリスチェシュアイアエアトンで65歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアエククレストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq(이)가 1477년 영국 체실르 이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Grosvenor(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine NORREYS(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 이턴에서 Katherine Cotton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 1621년도에 영국 체실르에서, 1621년도에 영국 체실르 c클스턴에서 살았다. 그는 1542년 7월 27일 영국 체실르 이턴에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 c클스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq nasceu em 1477, em Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Grosvenor, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Katherine NORREYS, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Cotton em 1495, em Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele viveu em Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1621 e em Eccleston, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1621. Ele faleceu em 27 de julho de 1542, em Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Eccleston, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq родился в 1477 г. в местности эатон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Grosvenor было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine NORREYS, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Cotton в 1495 г. в местности эатон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 9 дочерей. Он жил в местности чешир, Англия в 1621 году и в местности экклестон, чешир, Англия в 1621 году. Он умер 27 июля 1542 г. в местности эатон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности экклестон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Herbert Grosvenor, Esq nació en 1477, en Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Grosvenor, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Katherine NORREYS, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Katherine Cotton en 1495, en Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 9 hijas. Vivió en Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1621 y en Eccleston, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1621. Murió el 27 de julio de 1542, en Eaton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Eccleston, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
